name: Wallet Storage Adapter HTTP Interface — request/response contract

vectors.description: GET /settings — returns TableSettings with all required fields

expected.schema_note: TableSettings: { storageIdentityKey, storageName, chain, dbtype, maxOutputScript, created_at, updated_at }

vectors.description: GET /settings without auth token — returns 401 Unauthorized

expected.schema_note: ErrorResponse: { error: string, code?: string }

vectors.description: POST /migrate — returns storageName on successful migration
POST /actions — createAction with simple payment args returns StorageCreateActionResult

expected.schema_note: StorageCreateActionResult: { inputBeef?, inputs, outputs, noSendChangeOutputVouts?, derivationPrefix, version, lockTime, reference }

vectors.description: POST /actions with missing args field — returns 400 Bad Request
POST /actions/process — processAction stores a signed transaction and broadcasts

expected.schema_note: StorageProcessActionResults: { sendWithResults?, notDelayedResults?, log? }

vectors.description: POST /actions/abort — abort an unsigned action frees its inputs

expected.schema_note: AbortActionResult: { aborted: boolean }

vectors.description: POST /actions/internalize — internalize an external payment transaction

expected.schema_note: StorageInternalizeActionResult: { accepted, isMerge, txid, satoshis, sendWithResults?, notDelayedResults? }

vectors.description: POST /actions/internalize replay guard — duplicate tx returns isMerge: true
POST /list/actions — list wallet actions with default pagination
POST /list/outputs — list spendable UTXOs in a basket
POST /sync/chunk — export a sync chunk since a given timestamp

expected.schema_note: SyncChunk: { users?, transactions?, outputs?, outputBaskets?, provenTxs?, provenTxReqs?, syncStates? }

vectors.description: POST /sync/state — findOrInsertSyncState creates new state record on first call

expected.schema_note: FindOrInsertSyncStateResult: { syncState: TableSyncState, isNew: boolean }

vectors.description: POST /sync/active — setActive updates the user's active storage provider
POST /certificates — insert a new identity certificate
POST /certificates/relinquish — soft-delete a certificate
POST /outputs/relinquish — remove output from its basket
POST /outputs/relinquish with malformed outpoint — must return 400 Bad Request
GET /isStorageProvider — returns true for full StorageKnex/StorageIdb, false for the remoting client

expected.schema_note: boolean. Hosts that wrap remoting/HTTP clients MUST return false.

vectors.description: GET /available — returns true once makeAvailable has cached settings

expected.schema_note: boolean. Must be false before the first successful makeAvailable() call.

vectors.description: POST /makeAvailable — loads the singleton settings row and caches it for subsequent reads

expected.schema_note: TableSettings (same shape as GET /settings). Subsequent isAvailable() must return true.

vectors.description: POST /makeAvailable against an un-migrated database — returns 409 Conflict with a structured error

expected.schema_note: Implementations MUST refuse to fabricate a settings row; callers must invoke /migrate first.

vectors.description: GET /services — returns the configured WalletServices identity (chain + provider names)

expected.schema_note: WalletServicesInfo: { chain, broadcaster, chainTracker, merkleVerifier, lookupService }. Implementations that disable a service MUST return null for that key (not omit).

vectors.description: POST /auth — getAuth returns the resolved AuthId derived from the bearer token

expected.schema_note: AuthId: { identityKey, userId?, isActive? }. isActive=false means the user exists on this storage but a peer holds the active copy.

vectors.description: POST /users — findOrInsertUser inserts a new user and the default change basket on first call

expected.schema_note: FindOrInsertUserResult: { user: TableUser, isNew: boolean }. New users MUST receive a 'default' OutputBasket with numberOfDesiredUTXOs=144 and minimumDesiredUTXOValue=32. The default-constructed user.updated_at is the sync sentinel 1971-01-01T00:00:00.000Z so peers will overwrite it on first sync.

vectors.description: POST /users — second call for the same identity returns the existing user with isNew=false
POST /destroy — releases the underlying connection pool and resets isAvailable to false

expected.schema_note: After destroy, subsequent GET /available MUST return false. Calling destroy twice MUST be a no-op (still returns ok:true).

vectors.description: GET /stores — returns the active + backup storage providers known to the manager

expected.schema_note: WalletStorageInfo[]: { isActive, isEnabled, isBackup, isConflicting, userId, storageIdentityKey, storageName, storageClass, endpointURL? }. Exactly one entry MUST have isActive=true.

vectors.description: POST /find/certificates — partial match by certifier returns certificate rows with embedded fields

expected.schema_note: TableCertificateX[]: TableCertificate + optional fields[]. includeFields=true forces fields population; includeFields=false MUST omit the property.

vectors.description: POST /find/output-baskets — partial match by userId returns the default basket + any named baskets

expected.schema_note: TableOutputBasket[]: ordered by basketId ascending. Soft-deleted baskets (isDeleted=true) MUST be included unless the partial filters them out.

vectors.description: POST /find/outputs — partial match by userId+spendable=true with noScript=true

expected.schema_note: TableOutput[] (lockingScript MUST be omitted when noScript=true). When txStatus is provided, the underlying transaction's status MUST be in the supplied set.

vectors.description: POST /find/proven-tx-reqs — filter by an explicit status array and explicit txids

expected.schema_note: TableProvenTxReq[]. status array is OR-matched, txids array is AND-matched against the partial filter.

vectors.description: POST /list/certificates — auth-gated list returning embedded fields and total count

expected.schema_note: ListCertificatesResult: { totalCertificates, certificates: WalletCertificate[] }. fields is a flat map (BRC-100 shape), not an array — distinct from find/certificates which returns TableCertificateX rows.

vectors.description: POST /sync/chunk/process — apply a peer-supplied SyncChunk to the local store

expected.schema_note: ProcessSyncChunkResult: { done, maxUpdated_at, updates, inserts, error? }. done=true means the receiver has consumed every supplied row; the caller advances offsets and stops when both done=true and inserts+updates=0 from the next chunk.

vectors.description: POST /sync/chunk — non-empty chunk export contains outputs and transactions ordered by updated_at

expected.schema_note: SyncChunk: empty arrays MAY be omitted entirely. Rows MUST be returned in dependency order (offsets array) and within each entity sorted by updated_at ascending so the recipient can resume from a fixed cursor.

vectors.description: POST /list/actions with a label filter returns only matching actions and respects total count

expected.schema_note: labelQueryMode='any' OR-matches labels; 'all' AND-matches. Implementations MUST default to 'any' when omitted.

vectors.description: POST /list/outputs against an unknown basket — returns an empty list with totalOutputs=0

expected.schema_note: An unknown basket name is NOT an error — implementations MUST return an empty list. Throwing here is a conformance failure.

vectors.description: POST /actions — createAction with insufficient funds returns 422 with structured error

expected.schema_note: Implementations MUST surface totalSatoshis (the wallet's spendable balance considered) and requiredSatoshis. Currency-unit confusion is the most common cross-language regression here.

vectors.description: POST /actions/process — processAction with txid that does not match rawTx returns 400

body.error: txid does not match rawTx hash

vectors.description: POST /actions/abort — aborting an unknown reference returns 404
POST /actions/internalize — basket-insertion protocol stores the output under a named basket

body.description: Receive a BTMS token under the btms-tokens basket

expected.schema_note: InternalizeAction with protocol='basket insertion' MUST honour basket/tags/customInstructions on the resulting outputs row and MUST NOT consume change from the user's default basket.

vectors.description: POST /certificates — insert with a malformed revocationOutpoint returns 400
Any write endpoint with a bearer token that resolves to no AuthId returns 403 Forbidden

expected.schema_note: 401 means 'no credentials supplied'; 403 means 'credentials present but resolve to an unknown / inactive user'. Implementations MUST distinguish the two.
